title: Engine+Client ZK: enforce non-empty JOIN/BID proof payloads
description: Harden admission so JOIN and BID do not silently pass with null proof in production mode. Introduce an explicit enforcement mode (default off for backward compatibility if needed), reject missing proof when enabled, and update agent-client demo flow to submit real proof payloads instead of proof=null. Add tests for both compatibility mode and enforcement mode.
close_reason: ENGINE_REQUIRE_PROOFS=true rejects null proofs on JOIN (implemented by 1vy). BID proof is separate ticket vgg.

title: Write AuctionRegistry.sol
description: Auction state machine. DOMAIN_SEPARATOR (EIP712Domain 'AgentAuction', '1', chainId, address(this)). createAuction(auctionId, manifestHash, ...). recordResult(AuctionSettlementPacket, sequencerSig) — emits AuctionEnded. markSettled(auctionId) — onlyEscrow. cancelExpiredAuction(auctionId) — 72h timeout. updateWinnerWallet() — EIP-712 wallet rotation. State: NONE→OPEN→CLOSED→SETTLED (or CANCELLED). setEscrow(address) one-time binding. NO anchorHash. Ref: full_contract_arch(amended).md Section 7.
close_reason: AuctionRegistry.sol written (272 lines) and compiles clean. Full state machine: NONE→OPEN→CLOSED→SETTLED/CANCELLED. EIP-712 DOMAIN_SEPARATOR, recordResult with sequencer sig verification, markSettled (onlyEscrow), cancelExpiredAuction (72h), updateWinnerWallet (EIP-712 Domain 2).

title: Fix nullifier hash mismatch — trust ZK-proven Poseidon nullifier
description:
Fix nullifier hash mismatch: engine uses keccak256, ZK circuits use Poseidon — they never match. Engine should trust the ZK-proven nullifier from the proof instead of computing its own.

Supersedes the old Poseidon upgrade plan (plans/2026-02-27-poseidon-upgrade-plan.md) — that plan over-engineers the solution by changing the entire event hash chain. The event hash chain stays keccak (CF Workers compatible, just an integrity commitment). Only the nullifier tracking changes.

Maps to approved plan Steps 5-7.

## Root Cause
- engine/src/lib/crypto.ts deriveNullifier() uses keccak256(secret, auctionId, actionType)
- ZK circuit RegistryMembership uses Poseidon(agentSecret, auctionId, actionType)
- These produce different outputs → ZK nullifier from proof is never tracked
- Agent-client deriveJoinNullifier() also uses keccak256 (matches engine but not circuit)

## Engine Changes

### engine/src/lib/crypto.ts
- Remove deriveNullifier() function (keccak-based, lines ~90-106)
- verifyMembershipProof(): reject null proofs when ENGINE_REQUIRE_PROOFS=true (bug fix — currently returns valid:true for null)
- verifyMembershipProof(): add expectedRegistryRoot param, cross-check proof root against on-chain (bug fix — currently no root check)
- Return nullifier from proof publicSignals[2]

### engine/src/handlers/actions.ts
- handleJoin(): get nullifier from verifyMembershipProof() return, not from deriveNullifier()
- handleBid(): get nullifier from action request (agent includes it)
- Store ZK nullifier: checkNullifier uses proof-derived nullifier for double-join detection
- Add zkNullifierHash to ValidationMutation type

### engine/src/types/engine.ts
- Add zkNullifier?: string to AuctionEvent (privacy-ready field)

### engine/src/auction-room.ts
- Store zkNullifier in event when present
- Add to RoomSnapshot: highestBidderNullifier?: string

## Agent-Client Changes

### agent-client/src/auction.ts
- joinAuction(): when privateState provided, compute Poseidon nullifier via packages/crypto
- Include nullifier in action request payload
- deriveJoinNullifier(): keep as fallback for non-ZK mode (backward compat)

## NOT Changed
- Event hash chain stays keccak256 (CF Workers compatible, not ZK-verified)
- No per-auction hashAlgo field
- No Worker-safe Poseidon library needed in engine
- Solidity contracts unchanged

## Verify
- Engine crypto tests pass
- Engine typecheck + build
- Agent-client can generate and send nullifier that engine accepts
- Double-join with same ZK nullifier rejected
close_reason: Implemented: verifyMembershipProof with requireProof/expectedRegistryRoot options, ZK nullifier from publicSignals[2], zkNullifier stored in AuctionEvent, 4 new tests

Engine: roomConfig namespaced payload for extensibility (engine/future)
description:
Goal: make room config agent-friendly and forward-compatible by separating core engine-consumed params from extensible payload.

Scope:
1) Keep core auction params explicit (deadline/maxBid/snipe settings) for deterministic behavior and on-chain alignment.
2) Standardize room_config_json as namespaced object: { engine: {...}, future: {...} }.
3) Engine runtime reads only roomConfig.engine; unknown keys in roomConfig.future are persisted and surfaced but ignored by execution logic.
4) Keep backward compatibility for existing flat roomConfig payloads via migration/coercion at API layer.
5) Add tests for write/read compatibility and mechanics behavior under namespaced payload.

Files likely touched: engine/src/index.ts, engine/src/auction-room.ts, engine/src/types/engine.ts, engine/schema.sql, engine/test/api.test.ts, engine/test/auction-mechanics.test.ts.
notes:
Started implementation: standardizing roomConfig to namespaced engine/future shape with backward-compat coercion and tests.
Implemented namespaced roomConfig envelope support ({engine,future}) with backward-compatible flat payload coercion in create-auction and room init paths. Added tests for namespaced persistence + flat coercion and namespaced mechanics behavior. Validation: targeted tests and typecheck pass; full engine suite still has pre-existing failure in test/bond-watcher.test.ts.
close_reason: Implemented namespaced roomConfig {engine,future} with backward-compatible flat coercion and tests.

title: Auction mechanics: anti-sniping, maxBid cap, end-condition improvements
description:
Auction room mechanics: anti-sniping + maxBid + end-condition + close broadcast.

Four engine-side changes in auction-room.ts and actions.ts:

1. Anti-sniping (candle extension):
   - If a BID arrives within last SNIPE_WINDOW_SEC (60s) of deadline, extend deadline by EXTENSION_SEC (30s)
   - Max MAX_EXTENSIONS (5) extensions per auction
   - Update alarm schedule on extension
   - Persist extensionCount in DO storage
   - Broadcast deadline update to WebSocket clients
   - Config: SNIPE_WINDOW_SEC, EXTENSION_SEC, MAX_EXTENSIONS set per-auction at creation time (with sensible defaults)

2. maxBid enforcement:
   - auction creator sets optional maxBid in POST /auctions
   - Stored in D1 auctions.max_bid
   - actions.ts handleBid() checks: if maxBid > 0 && amount > maxBid → reject
   - Meaning: bond is a good-faith deposit, bid is willingness to pay, maxBid caps unrealistic bids

3. Auction end improvements:
   - No-bid auto-cancel: if closeAuction() finds zero BID events, set status=CANCELLED instead of CLOSED (skip recordResult)
   - Manual early close: POST /auctions/:id/close (sequencer-only auth) → calls closeAuction() immediately
   - Route cancelExpiredAuction: POST /auctions/:id/cancel for 72h timeout path

4. Close broadcast (NEW — currently missing):
   - closeAuction() currently does NOT notify agents when auction ends
   - Add broadcastEvent at end of closeAuction() with CLOSE event type:
     { actionType: "CLOSE", agentId: winnerAgentId, amount: winningBidAmount, wallet: winnerWallet }
   - If no-bid cancel: broadcast CANCEL event instead
   - This unblocks MCP watch_auction tool (q94) from returning AUCTION_ENDED to agents
   - Also add winner/result fields to GET /snapshot response so poll-based clients can see result

Design decisions (confirmed by user):
- English auction = public bids, no sealed-bid for MVP
- Bond = good-faith deposit, not full prepayment
- Anti-sniping via deadline extension (not random end or overtime)
- Close must notify agents with winner info (agent cannot maintain persistent WebSocket)
notes: Implemented engine-side anti-sniping, maxBid enforcement, close/cancel terminal broadcast, snapshot terminal fields, /close admin auth, /cancel timeout route, and extensible roomConfig payload support with tests.
close_reason: Implemented and validated engine-side anti-sniping, maxBid cap, close/cancel flow, terminal broadcast, and snapshot terminal fields.

title: x402 integration rollout (Phase 1 + Phase 2)
description:
Roll out x402 in a safe, agent-friendly way without changing core auction money rails.

Guardrails:
- Keep bond path primary as direct on-chain escrow transfer (EIP-4337/UserOp or EOA tx).
- Keep settlement/refund path on CRE + escrow, not x402.
- x402 is used as HTTP access/micropayment layer for auction APIs.

This epic tracks Phase 1 (platform-level read paywall hardening) and Phase 2 (per-auction policy overrides for read endpoints).
acceptance_criteria: - Phase 1 ticket delivered with tests and docs.\n- Phase 2 ticket delivered with schema migration, policy resolution logic, tests, and docs.\n- No regression to bond/settlement flows.
close_reason: Phase 1 and Phase 2 both implemented and pushed. Per-auction x402 policy overrides with schema, types, validation, resolution, dynamic middleware, PATCH endpoint, and 64 tests (27 unit + 37 integration).

title: Phase 1: x402 read paywall hardening (platform-level)
description:
Implement and harden platform-level x402 gating for read-heavy endpoints only.

Scope:
1) Keep x402-gated endpoints limited to:
   - GET /auctions/:id/manifest
   - GET /auctions/:id/events
2) Centralize gate policy in one resolver (mode + receiver + facilitator config) to avoid route drift.
3) Add robust observability: structured logs for 402 challenge issued / payment accepted / payment rejected.
4) Add receipt-dedup + idempotency instrumentation (do not double-serve paid call on duplicate receipt).
5) Keep explicit non-goal: do NOT gate bond/settlement/write-critical paths in this phase.

Files likely touched:
- engine/src/index.ts
- engine/src/middleware/x402.ts
- engine/test/api.test.ts (or dedicated x402 tests)
- docs (engine x402 behavior)
acceptance_criteria:
- With X402_MODE=off, manifest/events remain free and behavior unchanged.
- With X402_MODE=on, manifest/events require payment and return successful response after valid payment.
- Duplicate receipt attempts are handled idempotently (no double-processing).
- Bond and settlement routes remain non-x402 and pass existing tests.
- Tests cover on/off mode and basic payment lifecycle.
notes:
Implemented core Phase-1 changes in engine:
- centralized x402 gated route list + shared gate middleware
- fail-closed config validation when X402_MODE=on and receiver is missing/invalid
- structured x402 logs (challenge/accepted/rejected/duplicate)
- duplicate PAYMENT-SIGNATURE detection via x402_receipts hash check
- added helper module + tests (x402-policy.test.ts + api x402 cases)
Validation:
- npm run typecheck PASS
- focused x402 tests PASS
- full api.test.ts currently has unrelated pre-existing auth-route failures in this workspace
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: x402 gated routes, structured logging, receipt dedup, fail-closed config, 5/5 tests pass

title: Phase 2: per-auction x402 read policy (free/paid override)
description:
Add per-auction x402 policy override for read endpoints while preserving platform guardrails.

Scope:
1) Add D1 schema for per-auction policy (e.g., mode + optional price profile for read endpoints).
2) Policy resolution order:
   - platform defaults (admin config/env)
   - per-auction override (if present, within admin-allowed bounds)
3) Apply resolved policy to read endpoints only (manifest/events).
4) Add safe defaults and validation so creators cannot enable unsupported or abusive pricing.
5) Add admin/operator update path for policy changes (API or internal admin route), with audit logging.
6) Documentation updates: how auction owner policy interacts with platform defaults.

Non-goals:
- No x402 requirement on bond transfer, settlement, refund.
- No change to EIP-712 action schema or admission logic.
acceptance_criteria:
- Auction with no override follows platform default policy.
- Auction with override applies override only to manifest/events.
- Invalid override values are rejected with clear errors.
- Existing core auction flows (join/bid/bond/settlement) are unaffected.
- Tests cover default policy, override policy, and guardrail validation.
- Migration is forward-safe and documented.
close_reason: Phase 1 and Phase 2 both implemented and pushed. Per-auction x402 policy overrides with schema, types, validation, resolution, dynamic middleware, PATCH endpoint, and 64 tests (27 unit + 37 integration).

Remove ENGINE_ALLOW_INSECURE_STUBS dependency for signature path. This is secp256k1 signature verification, not ZK.

title: Engine: on-chain auction creation + agent onboard facilitator
description:
Engine on-chain auction creation + facilitator onboard endpoint + auction config fields.

Three changes to engine/:

1. POST /auctions: After writing to D1+DO, also call AuctionRegistry.createAuction() on-chain using sequencer key. Return txHash in response. Add new fields: title, description, auctionType (English/sealed-bid), maxBid (optional cap).

2. POST /onboard: New facilitator endpoint that:
   - Calls AgentAccountFactory.createAccount() to deploy smart wallet
   - Calls MockIdentityRegistry to register identity
   - Calls AgentPaymaster.registerAgent() (admin-only, engine has deployer key)
   - Mints MockUSDC to agent address
   - Returns deployed wallet address + status

3. D1 schema update: add title, description, auction_type, max_bid columns to auctions table.

Both 1 and 2 require engine to have sequencer/deployer private key (SEQUENCER_PRIVATE_KEY env var already in wrangler.toml).

title: Wire AgentPrivacyRegistry into ZK flow
description:
Wire the deployed AgentPrivacyRegistry (0x857E1049A5eE2cCA03a5C95F32089FECe51Ce8ff) into the ZK verification flow. This is Layer 1 (ZK Authorization) — not dependent on sealed-identity.

## Engine side (after hm3 + 1vy land)
- engine/src/lib/identity.ts: getPrivacyRegistryRoot() reads AgentPrivacyRegistry.getRoot()
- engine/src/lib/crypto.ts: verifyMembershipProof() cross-checks proof's registryRoot against on-chain root
- engine/src/handlers/actions.ts: verifyMembership() calls getPrivacyRegistryRoot() and passes to proof verifier

## Agent-client side (after hm3 lands)
- agent-client/src/privacy.ts (NEW): uses prepareOnboarding() from @agent-auction/crypto
- Agent generates agentSecret + salt locally, builds Poseidon Merkle tree

## Verify

- Proof with valid root passes
- Proof with stale/fake root rejected

title: Agent wallet adapters for Coinbase/Dynamic/Privy (Claude/Codex integration)
description:
Add a provider-agnostic wallet adapter layer so external AI agents (Claude Code / Codex) can use managed wallets instead of local private keys.

Context:
- Protocol path is already wallet-agnostic: Engine accepts signed actions at POST /auctions/:id/action and verifies EIP-712 signatures.
- Current implementation is demo-oriented and local-key based (agent-client signs with privateKeyToAccount; x402 helper also uses local private key).

Scope:
1) Define WalletSignerAdapter interface (minimum): signTypedData, signMessage, sendTx (or UserOp submit), getAddress.
2) Implement Coinbase CDP adapter first (server/delegated signing mode).
3) Add pluggable adapters for Dynamic and Privy with same interface (can be stubs behind feature flag if creds unavailable).
4) Refactor agent-client flow to use adapter abstraction (JOIN/BID signing and optional x402 signer source).
5) Keep Engine API unchanged for action verification compatibility.
6) Add onboarding challenge-sign design/flag (proof-of-control for runtimeSigner) for production hardening.

Non-goals:
- Changing EIP-712 schema or action endpoint contract.
- Binding protocol core to any single wallet provider.
acceptance_criteria:
- Agent can place JOIN/BID using adapter-backed signTypedData with no local raw private key usage in happy path.
- At least one provider adapter (Coinbase) verified end-to-end against /auctions/:id/action.
- Adapter interface supports adding Dynamic/Privy without call-site changes.
- Existing demo flow remains backward-compatible via local-key adapter fallback.
- Docs updated with integration guide and security model (delegated signing policies, key scope, rotation).
notes:
Implemented adapter abstraction in agent-client:
- Added WalletSignerAdapter + provider factory (local/coinbase/dynamic/privy) in agent-client/src/wallet-adapter.ts
- Refactored JOIN/BID signing and bond tx path to adapter methods in agent-client/src/auction.ts + agent-client/src/index.ts
- Updated x402 init to use adapter signer (no raw private key requirement in happy path) in agent-client/src/utils.ts
- Added onboarding challenge-sign flag ONBOARDING_CHALLENGE_SIGN for proof-of-control logging
- Updated docs/env guidance in agent-client/README.md

Validation: npm run typecheck (agent-client) passes.
Pending: live Coinbase E2E verification against /auctions/:id/action once CDP agent wallets + funded addresses are prepared.

title: Sealed-identity mode — nullifier-based routing (Phase 2, post-hackathon)
description:
DESCOPED for hackathon. Full sealed-identity refactor where agentId is removed from action payloads and engine routes by nullifier. Winner reveals identity at settlement via commit-reveal.

This is Layer 3 of the privacy architecture. Designed but not built for hackathon — the blast radius is too large (payloadHash, D1 schema, nonces, winner logic, replay bundle, CRE settlement, WebSocket, frontend).

## What we build instead (hackathon)
- 1vy: Fix ZK bugs (null proof, root check, nullifier tracking) — Layer 1
- Layer 2 prep is included in 1vy: store zkNullifier alongside agentId in events

## Phase 2 refactor scope (post-hackathon)
- ActionRequest: agentId optional, nullifier required in privacy mode
- computePayloadHash(): encode nullifier instead of agentId
- Nonce keys: nonce:{nullifier}:{actionType}
- Bond observations: keyed by (auctionId, nullifier)
- D1 schema migration: add nullifier column to events + bond_observations
- Winner determination: group by nullifier, reveal agentId at close
- Replay bundle: nullifier=0x... instead of agent_id=...
- CRE settlement: (auctionId, winnerNullifier, winnerWallet, amount)
- WebSocket broadcast: nullifier only, never agentId
- Frontend: leaderboard shows truncated nullifier
- Commit-reveal: DO storage identity:{nullifier}→{agentId, wallet} at JOIN, lookup at settlement

See approved plan Layer 3 table for full blast radius.

title: ZK privacy pipeline: circuits → proofs → engine verification → agent integration
description:
ZK privacy pipeline — broken into concrete steps:

STEP 1: Generate proving keys (.zkey) — BLOCKING everything else
  - Download powersOfTau28_hez_final_16.ptau
  - snarkjs groth16 setup RegistryMembership.r1cs → registry_member_0000.zkey
  - snarkjs zkey contribute → registry_member_final.zkey
  - Same for BidRange → bid_range_final.zkey
  - Verify: node test/test_membership.js && node test/test_bidrange.js (16 tests)

STEP 2: Fix packages/crypto tests (4/56 failing)
  - Root cause: proof-generator.ts references circuits/keys/*_final.zkey which don't exist
  - After Step 1, these should pass
  - Verify: cd packages/crypto && npm test → 56/56

STEP 3: Engine de-stub — replace verifyMembershipProof with real snarkjs.groth16.verify
  - Import vkey JSON directly (no fs needed in CF Workers)
  - Replace verifyMembershipProof stub with real groth16.verify
  - Remove ENGINE_ALLOW_INSECURE_STUBS dependency for ZK path
  - Verify: cd engine && npm run test → all pass

STEP 4: Deploy AgentPrivacyRegistry to Base Sepolia (overlaps pgi)
  - forge script script/Deploy.s.sol --sig 'step6b()'
  - Update deployments/base-sepolia.json
  - Verify on Basescan

NOTE: verifyEIP712Signature de-stub moved to z0k (not ZK-related, is secp256k1 signature verification).

Current state:
  ✅ Circom source (2 circuits)
  ✅ WASM (witness generation)
  ✅ Verification keys (vkey.json)
  ✅ Circuit tests (16/16 pass)
  ✅ Onboarding SDK (packages/crypto/onboarding.ts)
  ✅ Poseidon + nullifier (real in engine)
  ❌ Proving keys (.zkey) — MISSING
  ❌ Engine verifyMembershipProof — STUB
  ❌ AgentPrivacyRegistry — NOT DEPLOYED
close_reason: Decomposed into 4 subtasks: 6vb (generate .zkey), biq (fix crypto tests), 2yg (engine de-stub), 7pq (deploy AgentPrivacyRegistry)

title: Integrate real ERC-8004 Identity Registry (replace MockIdentityRegistry)
description:
Replace MockIdentityRegistry with real ERC-8004 Identity Registry. Agents self-register via register(agentURI) and own their agentId as ERC-721 NFT. Engine reads on-chain state only, never writes.

## Dependencies
- Do after auction-design-ltn (4337 cleanup removes /onboard + onboard.ts first)

## Engine Changes

### engine/src/lib/addresses.ts
- Add identityRegistry = 0x8004A818BFB912233c491871b3d84c89A494BD9e
- Rename mockIdentityRegistry → mockIdentityRegistryLegacy

### engine/src/lib/chain-client.ts
- Add erc8004RegistryAbi: ownerOf(uint256)→address, getAgentWallet(uint256)→address
- Add identityRegistry contract instance (read-only via publicClient)
- Add agentPrivacyRegistry contract instance for getRoot()→bytes32

### engine/src/lib/identity.ts (NEW)
- resolveAgentWallet(agentId): reads ownerOf or getAgentWallet from ERC-8004
- verifyAgentWallet(agentId, wallet): confirms wallet matches on-chain
- getPrivacyRegistryRoot(): reads AgentPrivacyRegistry.getRoot() for ZK verification

### engine/src/index.ts
- Add POST /verify-identity route (lightweight read-only check)
- Request: { agentId, wallet } → Response: { verified, resolvedWallet, privacyRegistered }
- Add ENGINE_VERIFY_WALLET and ENGINE_REQUIRE_PROOFS to Env type

### engine/src/handlers/actions.ts
- handleJoin(): if ENGINE_VERIFY_WALLET !== false, verify ownerOf(agentId) matches wallet
- Cache verified wallet in DO storage (walletVerified:{agentId})
- handleBid()/handleDeliver(): check cached wallet matches

## Agent-Client Changes

### agent-client/src/config.ts
- Add identityRegistry address + ERC-8004 ABI
- Add agentPrivacyRegistry address

### agent-client/src/identity.ts (REWRITE)
- Agent calls ERC8004.register(agentURI) themselves → parse agentId from Registered event
- No platform wallet creation, no admin minting

### agent-client/src/index.ts
- Update demo flow: agents self-register, no deployWallet

## Verify
- Engine typechecks + tests pass
- Agent-client typechecks
- cast call 0x8004... ownerOf(agentId) returns correct wallet

title: E2E integration test: full auction lifecycle on Base Sepolia
description:
End-to-end integration test on Base Sepolia:

Full lifecycle walkthrough to verify all components work together:
1. Human creates auction via frontend → engine → on-chain createAuction()
2. Agent onboards via POST /onboard → smart wallet + identity + paymaster + USDC
3. Agent bonds via agent-client → AuctionEscrow.depositBond()
4. Agent joins auction room (JOIN action with membership proof)
5. Agent places bids (BID action with range proof + x402 payment)
6. Deadline passes → engine alarm() → closeAuction() → recordResult on-chain
7. CRE triggers on AuctionEnded → onReport() → settlement
8. Winner claims, losers refund

This is the final verification before hackathon submission. Depends on all other Phase 1-2 beads.

Returns deployed wallet address + registration status. Engine uses deployer/sequencer key.

title: AuctionRegistry: replayContentHash event parameter emits manifestHash

Semantic mismatch — event consumers receive manifestHash labeled as replayContentHash. No current impact (skipReplayVerification=true) but will break if replay verification is enabled. Fix: rename event parameter or correctly store/emit replayContentHash.

title: MCP Server: auction tools for AI agents
description:
MCP Server: auction tools + strategy skills for AI agents.

TWO LAYERS:

=== Layer 1: Tools (操作能力) ===
  1. onboard_agent — 一键注册：Factory.createAccount + IdentityRegistry.register + Paymaster.registerAgent + USDC mint + (optional) PrivacyRegistry.register
     - Wraps agent-client/wallet.ts + identity.ts + crypto/onboarding.ts
     - Admin ops (registerAgent) done server-side with deployer key (方案A)
     - Returns: agentId, wallet address, USDC balance
  2. discover_auctions — GET /auctions, return list with title/reserve/deadline/status
  3. get_auction_details — GET /auctions/:id, return snapshot + config + current highest bid
  4. deposit_bond — USDC.transfer to escrow (via UserOp if EIP-4337, or direct EOA)
  5. join_auction — POST /auctions/:id/action (JOIN), auto-generates ZK membership proof
  6. place_bid — POST /auctions/:id/action (BID), takes amount
     - Response ALWAYS includes latest auction state (highest bid, leader, timeRemaining)
     - On rejection: includes reason + current highest + suggestion
  7. get_my_status — bond status + bid history + auction result
  8. claim_refund — AuctionEscrow.claimRefund() on-chain
  9. watch_auction — long-poll with timeout (default 10s)
     - MCP server holds WebSocket to engine, buffers events
     - Blocks until new event arrives or timeout
     - Returns: new events[] + current snapshot
     - LLM agent calls this to "listen for a bit" between decisions

=== Layer 2: Skills / Prompts (策略知识) ===
  1. auction_rules — Explain English auction rules, what reserve price means, what bond is for
  2. bidding_strategy — How to decide bid amount: observe current highest, consider reserve, stay within budget
  3. risk_assessment — Bond vs reward analysis, when to walk away
  4. onboarding_guide — Step-by-step for first-time agents
  5. auction_lifecycle — What happens after deadline (settlement, refund, delivery)
  6. participation_loop — Teaches agent the correct observe→think→act cycle:
     - Call watch_auction(timeout=5s) to observe
     - Analyze: am I leading? how much time left? who is bidding?
     - Decide: raise, hold, or withdraw
     - Call place_bid if needed
     - Repeat until auction ends

=== LLM Agent Interaction Design ===
  Problem: LLM agents are synchronous tool callers — they cannot maintain
  persistent WebSocket connections or receive push notifications while thinking.
  
  Solution (three-layer approach):
  1. Every tool response piggybacks latest auction state — agent gets updates
     passively with zero extra cost on every interaction
  2. watch_auction long-poll — agent actively chooses to "listen for a while",
     MCP server returns immediately when new event arrives (or on timeout)
  3. participation_loop skill — teaches agent the correct behavioral pattern
     so it knows to poll between decisions
  
  MCP server maintains persistent WebSocket to engine internally,
  buffers events, and serves them to agent via synchronous tool calls.

=== Transport ===
  Streamable HTTP (MCP spec) — single endpoint
  
=== x402 Integration ===
  MCP server handles x402 internally:
  - Agent provides wallet/key at connection time
  - Server signs EIP-712 authorization from agent's wallet for gated endpoints
  - Agent doesn't need to know about x402

=== Auth ===
  Agent provides private key (or wallet config) at MCP connection setup.
  Server uses deployer key for admin ops (registerAgent, etc.)

=== Existing Code to Reuse ===
  - agent-client/wallet.ts → deployWallet()
  - agent-client/identity.ts → registerIdentity(), fundWithUSDC()
  - packages/crypto/onboarding.ts → prepareOnboarding(), registerOnChain()
  - packages/crypto/proof-generator.ts → generateMembershipProof(), generateBidRangeProof()

=== Location ===
  New directory: mcp-server/

=== Demo Vision ===
  Claude Code connects to MCP server → "help me find an auction and bid on it" →
  agent autonomously onboards, bonds, joins, watches, bids, monitors, claims.

title: x402 real payment: EIP-712 signed USDC authorization + on-chain transfer
description: Implement real x402 payment in engine/src/middleware/x402.ts. Agent signs EIP-712 typed data authorizing USDC spend (amount, nonce, expiry, recipient=engine). Engine verifies signature via viem verifyTypedData, submits on-chain USDC transferFrom, tracks nonce for replay prevention. Requires deployed engine + funded agents. Do after deployment.

title: DeployNftEscrow script — Base Sepolia deployment

title: Engine API — POST /auctions/:id/image upload to IPFS
description: New endpoint: POST /auctions/:id/image. Accept multipart form or raw binary. Upload to Pinata via pinToIpfs, store CID in D1 item_image_cid column. 10MB limit.
close_reason: POST /auctions/:id/image route exists (lines 664-714) with multipart form + raw body + IPFS pinning.

close_reason: Closed

title: P1 ZK integrity: include proof bytes in replay bundle and CRE re-verify winner proofs
description: Implement documented P1 mitigation for off-chain ZK trust gap. Extend replay bundle format to include proof bytes and public signals for relevant actions, then add CRE settlement-side verification of at least winner JOIN/BID proofs before report write. Keep backward compatibility or version replay schema explicitly. Add integration tests for proof-present success and proof-tampered failure.

title: Write Deploy.s.sol deployment script for all 6 contracts + testnet mocks

title: Engine ZK: verify BidRange proofs on BID actions
description: Add BidRange proof verification in BID validation path. On BID, validate supplied Groth16 proof using snarkjs.groth16.verify and bid range verification key, enforce rangeOk output, and bind public inputs to current auction parameters (reserve price / budget semantics as defined by circuit). Reject malformed or invalid proofs fail-closed. Add focused tests for valid proof acceptance and invalid proof rejection.

title: AuctionEscrow: Call configureCRE() to pin CRE workflow credentials
description: MEDIUM severity. expectedWorkflowId/expectedWorkflowName/expectedAuthor default to zero — if configureCRE() is never called, onReport() skips all CRE metadata validation. Any registered CRE workflow on the DON could submit fabricated settlement reports. Fix: call configureCRE() immediately after deployment. Consider adding require(expectedWorkflowId != bytes32(0)) guard in onReport() for defense-in-depth.
close_reason: configureCRE() called on AuctionEscrow with mock values (TX 0x3313e228). Mock values match cre workflow simulate defaults. Real values will be set after d26 (cre workflow deploy) completes. ConfigureCRE.s.sol Foundry script created for future use.

title: x402 real payment integration
description: Replace stub x402 middleware with real @x402/hono (engine) and @x402/fetch (agent-client) for HTTP 402 micropayments on Base Sepolia
close_reason: Split into granular sub-tasks

title: MEDIUM: Add workflowName validation in onReport
close_reason: Fixed: onReport validates bytes10 workflowName from metadata. Skips check if expectedWorkflowName is bytes10(0). Tests verify both paths.

title: Deploy: engine to CF Workers + frontend to Vercel
description:
Deploy infrastructure to make the platform publicly accessible:

1. Engine → Cloudflare Workers:
   - wrangler d1 create auction-engine-db
   - wrangler d1 execute --file=schema.sql
   - wrangler secret put SEQUENCER_PRIVATE_KEY
   - npm run deploy (wrangler deploy)
   - Verify: curl https://<worker>.workers.dev/auctions

2. Frontend → Vercel (or CF Pages):
   - Set NEXT_PUBLIC_ENGINE_URL env var
   - Deploy
   - Verify: visit deployed URL, confirm /auctions loads

Both are human tasks requiring credentials, but tracking together since they're the same milestone: platform goes live.
close_reason: Engine deployed to CF Workers, frontend deployed to Vercel

title: Write AuctionEscrow.sol
description: Core escrow contract. Inherits ReceiverTemplate + ReentrancyGuard. recordBond(auctionId, agentId, depositor, amount, x402TxId) — onlyAdmin, idempotent on txId. _processReport(bytes report) — decode abi.encode(auctionId, winnerAgentId, winnerWallet, amount), O(1) settlement. claimRefund(auctionId, agentId) — pull-based, deposit-time beneficiary. withdraw() — pull pattern. adminRefund() — emergency. checkSolvency() — view. Solvency invariant: usdc.balanceOf(this) >= totalBonded + totalWithdrawable. Ref: full_contract_arch(amended).md Section 8.
close_reason: AuctionEscrow.sol written (305 lines) and compiles clean. Implements IReceiver for CRE settlement. recordBond with idempotency (txHash+logIndex), onReport metadata validation, _processReport settlement, pull-based claimRefund, withdraw, adminRefund emergency. Solvency check view.

title: Agent client — createAuction with NFT metadata + uploadAuctionImage
description: Extend agent-client/src/auction.ts createAuction to accept title, description, itemImageCid, nftContract, nftTokenId, nftChainId. Add uploadAuctionImage function for POST /auctions/:id/image.

title: x402 real payment: EIP-712 signed authorization verification
description:
Implement real x402 payment verification in engine/src/middleware/x402.ts:

Current state: X402_MODE=off bypasses all payment. insecure_header_hash mode only checks receipt uniqueness via sha256 — no actual USDC payment.

Target: EIP-712 signed authorization model:
1. Agent signs EIP-712 typed data authorizing USDC spend (amount, nonce, expiry, recipient=engine)
2. Engine verifies signature via viem verifyTypedData
3. Engine submits on-chain transfer (or calls approve+transferFrom) to collect USDC
4. Engine tracks nonce to prevent replay

Also includes de-stubbing verifyEIP712Signature in engine/src/lib/crypto.ts (moved from g3r — this is secp256k1 signature verification, not ZK):
  - Replace always-true stub with real viem.verifyTypedData
  - Used by JOIN and BID action validation in handlers/actions.ts
  - Remove ENGINE_ALLOW_INSECURE_STUBS dependency for signature path
close_reason: Decomposed into 4sy (EIP-712 de-stub, can do now) and qqo (x402 real payment, after deployment)

title: Write AgentAccount.sol
description: EIP-4337 smart contract wallet for agents. Inherits BaseAccount, implement _validateSignature(): ecrecover(userOpHash, sig) == runtimeSigner. Store address public runtimeSigner (set at creation, rotatable via setRuntimeSigner). execute() callable only by EntryPoint. NO ZK verification (moved to DO). Ref: full_contract_arch(amended).md Section 3.
close_reason: AgentAccount.sol written: BaseAccount + Initializable, runtimeSigner EOA validation via ecrecover, execute/executeBatch, setRuntimeSigner rotation, all lint clean. 95 lines.

title: Engine ZK: enforce membership registryRoot against AgentPrivacyRegistry
description: Implement on-chain registry root binding for membership proofs in the DO sequencer. Current verifyMembershipProof only checks cryptographic validity of Groth16 proof and does not compare publicSignals[0] with AgentPrivacyRegistry.getRoot(). Add RPC read of getRoot(), compare against proof registryRoot, reject mismatches, and keep fail-closed behavior. Include tests for valid root pass and stale/fake root rejection.
